claim_boundary: Tier 4.30 is a local engineering contract. It does not implement the C runtime lifecycle surface, does not run hardware, does not prove lifecycle/self-scaling, does not freeze a lifecycle baseline, does not migrate v2.2 temporal state, and does not claim speedup or multi-chip scaling.

command_schema.deterministic_rule: initial active slots are 0..founder_count-1; all other slots inactive
command_schema.forbidden_behavior: no dynamic allocation, no graph rebuild, no implicit extra founders

command_schema.deterministic_rule: event is applied only if invariants hold; invalid events increment invalid_event_count
command_schema.forbidden_behavior: no silent overwrite of active child slot, no lineage mutation without event telemetry
command_schema.payload_fields: event_index, event_type, parent_slot, target_slot, child_slot, trophic_delta_raw, reward_raw
command_schema.phase: local reference and first hardware smoke

command_schema.deterministic_rule: trophic_health, cyclin_d, and bax update in fixed-point with explicit clipping
command_schema.forbidden_behavior: no float-only host trophic decision in hardware claim
command_schema.phase: after lifecycle smoke, before task-effect claims

command_schema.deterministic_rule: readback schema is stable and versioned; compact summary always available
command_schema.forbidden_behavior: no paper claim from unversioned or partial lifecycle state
command_schema.required_ack: schema_version, pool_summary, per-slot compact rows or declared truncation

command_schema.deterministic_rule: control perturbation is seeded and reproduced exactly by local reference
command_schema.forbidden_behavior: no post-hoc relabeling of failed lifecycle runs as controls

hardware_package_rule.allowed_after: contract pass, local reference pass, source audit pass, generated package contains source only
hardware_package_rule.first_hardware_scope: single-core active-mask and lineage telemetry smoke

forbidden: legacy SDRAM malloc/free neuron_birth/neuron_death as lifecycle proof
layering.software_reference_rule: v2.2 informs future comparisons but is not migrated into hardware lifecycle in Tier 4.30.

local_reference.event_count: 32 canonical events plus optional 64-event boundary smoke
local_reference.language: Python deterministic reference first; C fixed-point parity second
local_reference.seed_rule: seed 42 for first local reference; seeds 42/43/44 only after deterministic seed-42 pass
local_reference.tolerance: exact for integer/mask/lineage/event counters; <=1 raw unit for fixed-point summaries unless predeclared otherwise

pool.capacity_model: fixed compile-time pool; lifecycle events activate, silence, or reassign slots
pool.first_task_scope: state mechanics only; task-effect claims begin only after local and hardware state parity

criteria.name: layering decision imported
criteria.rule: uses native mechanism bridge with v2.2 software reference only

criteria.name: static runtime capacity sufficient for first reference
legacy dynamic birth/death excluded
criteria.note: The legacy path exists but is explicitly not the Tier 4.30 proof.

criteria.name: lifecycle surface not already silently present
local reference required before hardware
criteria.value: contract pass, local reference pass, source audit pass, generated package contains source only

criteria.name: claim boundary forbids unsupported jumps
value: legacy SDRAM malloc/free neuron_birth/neuron_death as lifecycle proof

event_semantics.required_invariants: slot active; fixed-point values clipped; no lineage mutation
parent remains active; child was inactive; event increments exactly once
event_semantics.state_updates: child active, child parent_slot, child generation, child lineage_id, cleavage_count
event_semantics.trigger_inputs: parent active, inactive child slot available, cyclin gate passes

event_semantics.required_invariants: adult gate explicit; no dynamic allocation; no hidden extra capacity
event_semantics.trigger_inputs: adult parent active, inactive child slot available, trophic and maturity gates pass

event_semantics.required_invariants: active_count decreases by one; lineage remains readable after death
event_semantics.state_updates: slot inactive, death_count, final lineage telemetry preserved
event_semantics.trigger_inputs: active slot, bax/death gate passes or explicit reference event

event_semantics.required_invariants: no active-mask change unless paired with accepted birth/cleavage/death
event_semantics.state_updates: last_event_type, maturity marker encoded through cyclin/trophic state
event_semantics.trigger_inputs: age threshold or trophic threshold reached

failure_classes.meaning: A command, field, control, artifact, or claim boundary is not specified.
failure_classes.required_response: Do not implement runtime code; repair the contract first.

failure_classes.meaning: The proposed lifecycle proof depends on dynamic graph creation or malloc/free neuron birth/death.
failure_classes.required_response: Reject as noncanonical for Tier 4.30; redesign as static pool/mask.

failure_classes.meaning: C/local candidate does not match the deterministic Python reference.
Hardware emits a state schema the ingest/parser cannot verify.
failure_classes.required_response: Stop and repair schema/versioning; no scientific claim.

failure_classes.meaning: Active mask, lineage checksum, parent links, or event counters diverge.
failure_classes.required_response: Classify as lifecycle-state failure and debug before controls.

failure_classes.meaning: Fixed capacity, random events, shuffled masks, shuffled lineage, or no-trophic controls match the enabled path.
failure_classes.required_response: Do not promote lifecycle-native baseline; narrow organism claim or redesign mechanism.

failure_classes.meaning: A report claims lifecycle superiority, multi-chip scaling, speedup, or native v2.2 temporal state from this contract.
failure_classes.required_response: Reject report wording and correct source-of-truth docs.

gate_sequence.artifacts: results JSON, report MD, command/readback/event/gate/failure CSVs
gate_sequence.fail_criteria: ambiguous ownership, missing shams, dynamic allocation dependency, or unscoped v2.2 migration
gate_sequence.pass_criteria: all readiness inputs pass; command/readback/event/gate/failure schemas complete

gate_sequence.artifacts: local reference JSON, per-event CSV, final state CSV, control summaries
gate_sequence.fail_criteria: any nondeterminism, hidden capacity growth, invalid events in canonical pass
gate_sequence.pass_criteria: exact active mask/lineage/event/checksum parity and all controls precomputed
gate_sequence.scope: 8-slot pool, 2 founders, 32-64 lifecycle events, no hardware

gate_sequence.artifacts: hardware results JSON, report MD, board/core info, compact readback, ingest report
gate_sequence.fail_criteria: readback schema mismatch, stale state, dynamic allocation, timeout, or lineage checksum mismatch
gate_sequence.pass_criteria: real hardware, zero fallback, compact readback matches local reference within fixed-point tolerance
gate_sequence.scope: active-mask and lineage telemetry only, one seed first

gate_sequence.artifacts: core map, message counters, compact readback, local/hardware diff
gate_sequence.fail_criteria: cross-core stale reply, timeout, corrupted lineage, or unmatched event count
gate_sequence.pass_criteria: no stale replies, no mask corruption, local parity preserved across cores
gate_sequence.scope: lifecycle state split from learning/context/route/memory cores

gate_sequence.artifacts: control matrix CSV, effect sizes, per-seed rows, failure classification
gate_sequence.fail_criteria: shams match lifecycle, no task effect, or lineage telemetry is decorative
gate_sequence.pass_criteria: lifecycle-enabled path separates from shams on a predeclared lifecycle-pressure task
gate_sequence.scope: fixed pool, random events, mask shuffle, lineage shuffle, no trophic, no dopamine/plasticity

readback_schema.pass_rule: stable unless slot is reassigned by accepted event
-1 for founders or valid parent slot
increments only on accepted child event
matches final accepted event for slot
